page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton processor using the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.5 GHz, supported by 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. It includes a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates that the single-core processing capabilities of the r6gd.medium are moderate, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1471.08, placing it in the middle tier of tested servers. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are limited due to the single-core configuration, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1471.08 and a large-block read memory bandwidth of 19.08 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware AES acceleration, though other extended instructions like AVX are absent. The instance achieves 1.13 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and 1.17 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6gd.medium offers a resource-dense entry point for memory-intensive workloads that require local NVMe storage without the overhead of larger multi-core instances. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local SSD storage make it suitable for lightweight database hosting, caching layers like Redis, basic web serving, and memory-bound development tasks that do not require high parallel processing power.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.medium is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, and includes a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers dedicated CPU allocation. Benchmark results show moderate single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1471.08, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reflect its single-vCPU limitation. The instance achieves 1.13 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 1.17 GB/sec in static web serving. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads requiring local SSD storage, such as lightweight caching layers, small databases, and basic web serving, where high parallel processing is not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.medium is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 1 vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for lightweight caching and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.medium is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 1 vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD for lightweight database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring dedicated single-core compute and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD.
Delivers 1.13 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation.
Best suited for lightweight caching, small databases, and basic web serving.
